result:
If I had to choose one thing, I'd want everyone to have genuine curiosity about perspectives different from their own - not just tolerance, but real interest in understanding how others see the world.

I think so many problems stem from our tendency to assume our own viewpoint is complete or obviously correct. With more curiosity, people might ask "why do you see it that way?" instead of immediately arguing. They might discover surprising common ground, or at least understand disagreements more deeply.

It wouldn't solve everything, but I think it could ripple outward - affecting how we approach political divides, cultural differences, scientific debates, and everyday conflicts. Even just a bit more "I wonder why they think that" could go a long way.

What change would you make?

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
